items.title: The US Army is already ditching its most powerful laser weapon yet
items.desc: The U.S. Army’s 300 kilowatt Indirect Fire Protection Capability-High Energy Laser system won't transition to a program of record.

items.title: Texas Education Agency orders public schools to remove mentions of Cesar Chavez from lessons
items.desc: Schools were also directed to cancel or change celebrations of the late civil rights leader amid allegations of sexual abuse.

items.title: Supreme Court rejects appeal from Texas death row inmate Rodney Reed
items.desc: Reed has sought to test crime-scene evidence that he says will help clear him of the killing of 19-year-old Stacey Stites in 1996.

items.title: Blocked from Texas vouchers, this private Islamic school wants a chance to prove its pro-America values
items.desc: Several Islamic schools sued Texas for excluding them from the voucher program. Iman Academy is instead calling for fairness while hoping to be judged by its work — not stereotypes.

items.title: Feds plan to install 536 miles of floating barriers on Rio Grande to deter migrants
items.desc: The government is installing the first 17 miles of industrial-grade buoys in Brownsville. Experts warn the buoys could intensify flooding and change the river’s course.

items.title: Why Texas’ Population Boom Is Reshaping Local Mobility
items.desc: Rapid growth across Texas is changing how families move, settle, and build community.

items.title: Aramco CEO Withdraws From Houston Event
items.desc: Saudi Aramco Chief Executive Officer Amin Nasser has withdrawn from a major energy conference in Houston as the situation in the Middle East remains volatile.

items.title: AEP, SoftBank Ink Deal with US DOE for 10 GW Data Center Power
items.desc: The mostly gas generation capacity will serve a 10-GW data center planned to rise at the site of a former gaseous diffusion plant in Pike County, Ohio.

items.title: Trump Reports 'Productive Conversations' with Iran
items.desc: 'I have instructed the Department of War to postpone any and all military strikes against Iranian power plants and energy infrastructure for a five day period', Trump said.

items.title: QatarEnergy Missile Strikes May Cause $100B Revenue Loss
items.desc: QatarEnergy said it expects the damage to its Ras Laffan Industrial City caused by missile strikes to cost about $20 billion a year in lost revenue and to take up to five years to repair.

items.title: Iberdrola Reaches Over 11 GW of Installed Capacity in US
items.desc: 'Avangrid's growing portfolio is helping to meet a surge in energy demand across the country brought on by the proliferation of data centers powering the country's AI revolution'.

items.title: OpenAI in Talks with Helion to Secure Fusion Energy

items.title: Nuclear Sprint: DOE and Industry Race to Meet Trump’s Target

items.title: JenaValve snags FDA approval in aortic regurgitation
items.desc: The approval comes two months after Edwards Lifesciences scuttled plans to buy JenaValve for $945 million amid opposition from the Federal Trade Commission.

items.title: California sues Department of Energy over restarted oil pipelines
items.desc: The state claims the federal government unlawfully invoked the Defense Production Act to reopen two pipelines around Santa Barbara, California.

items.title: Kennedy Center board sued over planned closure and reconstruction
items.desc: The board, chaired by President Donald Trump, approved a proposal to shut the nation’s premier cultural center for two years and remodel the building under the president’s direction.

items.title: Open the Portal Talks ‘Smiling Friends’ and 11 Years of Mixed Media
items.desc: The studio co-founders applied years of stop-motion expertise to create 7 minutes of a “magnificently disturbing mash-up of bright 2D and creepy Claymation” in a special episode featuring Mother Nature on the hit Adult Swim comedy.

items.title: Oscar Nominee Konstantin Bronzit’s 'Tied Up' Sets Worldwide Digital Release
items.desc: Feature film by the ‘The Three Sisters’ director and ‘Lavatory Lovestory’ writer Dmitry Vysotsky about an unlikely group of discarded objects on a journey of purpose and belonging, releases March 24 across most major digital platforms.

items.title: Disney Drops ‘Moana’ Live-Action Official Trailer
items.desc: Catherine Lagaʻaia stars as Moana alongside Dwayne Johnson as Maui, the ‘shape shifter, demigod of the wind and sea, hero of men... and women too’ in the upcoming adaptation of the studio’s hit animated film; in theaters July 10.

items.title: MoMI’s 5th Annual Marvels of Media Festival Set for March 26–28
items.desc: The Museum of the Moving Image will host the expansive Astoria, NY event that features work created by autistic media makers, including films, a video game, VR experiences, and expanded nationwide theater partnerships.

items.title: ‘Mickey + Bluey’ Animated Short Debuts on Disney+
items.desc: The new crossover video brings Mickey Mouse and the famed Blue Heeler together on screen for the first time as part of Disney’s collaboration with Ludo Studio and BBC Studios.
